premise: My attorney brother convinced me to let him 'handle' our parents’ $450,000 life insurance after they died in a crash, then he forged my signature on the release, cashed the check, quietly paid off the family farm’s $300,000 mortgage, took sole title, and evicted me. I later became a licensed insurance investigator, uncovered the forgery, sued, got a $500,000 judgment plus the farm, and now the farmhouse is leased to an organic co-op that pays me a steady share—while he’s permanently disbarred.
